channel.title: ICYMI Law — AI & The Law Link Feed
channel.description: Popular links about AI and the law shared recently on Bluesky.

item.title: Lawsuit: Man used Grok to make 7K sex images of stepdaughter, then shot himself
item.description:
More young girls sue X over Grok CSAM; X accused of shielding child predators.

🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 596 · likes: 1598

item.title: AI software that generates ‘rage bait’ developed by Germany’s far-right AfD
item.description:
Alternita platform uses Google Gemini, OpenAI and Claude to create provocative social media posts

🔥 · reposts: 133 · likes: 253

item.title: Lawsuit: Grok user made 7K child sex images; xAI only reported one gang rape prompt
item.description:
More young girls sue X over Grok CSAM; X accused of shielding child predators.

reposts: 44 · likes: 122

item.title: UK regulator warns of "arms race" to keep up with AI use in financial services
item.description:
FCA official makes case for greater powers for watchdog as millions use technology for personal finance decisions.

🔥 · reposts: 6 · likes: 33

item.title: AI startup CEO pleaded guilty in US to trading on insider tips from lawyers
item.description:
The founder ​and chief executive of an AI startup secretly pleaded guilty last year to participating in ‌a vast scheme in which attorneys at major law firms tipped traders about mergers their employers were advising on.

🔥 · reposts: 10 · likes: 11

item.title: Nearly half of early career lawyers use AI daily, survey finds
item.description:
Most early career lawyers are using AI in their work — primarily to conduct legal research and compile information, a new survey has found.

🔥 · reposts: 7 · likes: 15

item.title: Why We Need a 'Truth Campaign' for the AI Era
item.description:
A massive public education campaign is necessary to equip everyone with the knowledge and means to live with AI, write Gaurav Laroia and Charlotte Slaiman.

🔥 · reposts: 5 · likes: 18

item.title: ‘Killer Robots’ Must Be Banned, U.N. Secretary-General Says
item.description:
António Guterres labels lethal autonomous weapons “morally repugnant,” resurfacing issue that was central to Anthropic’s clash with the Pentagon.

reposts: 17 · likes: 52

item.title: Wildfires rage across southern Europe, forcing thousands to flee homes
item.description:
Tour de France spectator ban as country along with Spain, Portugal and Greece faces ‘powder keg’ after heatwave

reposts: 17 · likes: 30

item.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage | TechCrunch
item.description:
As part of an ongoing legal dispute with three Hollywood studios, Midjourney is seeking to compel those studios to reveal how they use AI themselves.

reposts: 8 · likes: 58

item.title: A Judicial Wake-Up Call on Government by AI
item.description:
As agencies craft best practices for LLM use, the Learned Societies decision should serve as a handbook of what not to do, writes Jordan Ascher.

reposts: 6 · likes: 16

item.title: Read the Tense Emails Between the Pentagon (Former Uber Exec) and Anthropic (Dario Amodei)
item.description:
Here's the full court document showing what went on behind the scenes before the Pentagon turned on Anthropic.

YouTube video by Rhett Shull

item.title: Power to the People
item.description:
Modernizing our aging energy grid will be a high-voltage test for America's 250-year-old democracy.

item.title: Behind the Blog: With Blogs Like These, Who Needs a Private Jet
item.description:
This week, we discuss the Supreme Court, the private jet, and AI on the TV.

item.title: Opinion | Is the future of AI open?
item.description:
Always be wary when companies ask government to get more involved in their industry.

item.description:
As agencies craft best practices for LLM use, the Learned Societies decision should serve as a handbook of what not to do, writes Jordan Ascher.

item.title: Creatives sound alarm on copyright as Pocock calls $50bn datacentre proposal ‘ultimate dirty deal’
item.description:
Proposal has been put to cabinet to allow AI companies to mine content, in exchange for investment and $350m fund to compensate artists, sources say

🔥 · reposts: 24 · likes: 61

item.title: It reads your email, files your claims, and never asks permission — The privacy law of AI agents
item.description:
Sara H. Jodka of Dickinson Wright PLLC discusses the flow of information through AI agents often without human sign-off and the privacy risks and compliance issues raised.

reposts: 18 · likes: 26

item.title: US Law Enforcement Warns of ‘Anti-Tech Extremism’ as AI Hatred Grows
item.description:
As Americans stew over the looming risk of job-stealing AI and data centers in their back yards, the feds are raising the alarm about a new category of threat, documents obtained by WIRED show.

🔥🔥 · reposts: 20 · likes: 64

item.title: Creatives sound alarm on copyright as Pocock calls $50bn datacentre proposal ‘ultimate dirty deal’
item.description:
Proposal has been put to cabinet to allow AI companies to mine content, in exchange for investment and $350m fund to compensate artists, sources say

item.title: California man with bipolar disorder says ChatGPT fueled delusions, led to self-harm in new lawsuit
item.description:
A California man sued OpenAI and its ​CEO Sam Altman on Wednesday, claiming the company's ChatGPT platform exacerbated his bipolar disorder due to a lack of ‌safeguards for users with mental illness.

item.title: Bernie Sanders Saw This Coming
item.description:
For decades, Bernie Sanders has argued that concentrated wealth threatened American democracy. Now he’s betting that frustration with Big Tech, billionaires, and unchecked AI is reaching a tipping poi...

item.title: U.S. approach to regulation of AI is problematic, Sixth Street's Chavez says
item.description:
The United States' regulation of artificial intelligence is problematic and inconsistent, Martin Chavez, vice chairman at investment firm Sixth ​Street, told Reuters on Tuesday.

🔥 · reposts: 3 · likes: 12

item.title: NYT slams Microsoft for building copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI
item.description:
NYT shifts OpenAI/Microsoft copyright claims after SCOTUS ruling against Sony.

item.title: Ministers likely to support law change to allow delivery robots on England’s paths
item.description:
Exclusive: Safety campaigners concerned about plan for widespread deployment on already crowded pavements

item.title: Meta Contractors Posed as Teens to Prompt Rival Chatbots About Suicide, Sex, and Drugs
item.description:
Hundreds of contractors working on a project for Meta pretended to be kids in order to see how other chatbots like Gemini and ChatGPT would respond to high-risk subjects, WIRED found.

reposts: 5 · likes: 16

item.title: If you thought the global financial crisis was bad…
item.description:
When the crash comes, stabilising markets will be easy compared with reordering society for AI, writes short-seller Carson Block

item.title: Ethical AI rows open way to wave of litigation
item.description:
Lawyers are at the forefront of debate and dispute over the technology’s lawful and responsible use

item.title: Lawmakers Must Act Now to Prevent Armed Police Drones
item.description:

item.title: Microsoft built supercomputer to help OpenAI infringe copyrights, NYT alleged
item.description:
NYT shifts OpenAI/Microsoft copyright claims after SCOTUS ruling against Sony.

item.title: OpenAI, Microsoft Sued by Publishers for Scraping Articles (1)
item.description:
Publishers that collectively own and operate nearly 400 newspapers are suing OpenAI Inc. and Microsoft Corp. for scraping their content to build products like ChatGPT and Microsoft Copilot without permission or compensation.

item.title: Kansas City bus agency wants to use AI facial recognition. Critics say it'll make riders less safe
item.description:
The Kansas City Area Transportation Authority could be one of the first in the nation to add AI-powered facial recognition cameras to its bus fleet this fall. National nonprofits and a KCATA commissio...

reposts: 50 · likes: 139

item.title: Nearly 400 local newspapers sue OpenAI, Microsoft over alleged copyright theft - New Jersey Globe
item.description:
The massive coalition of local newspaper publishers filed a federal lawsuit today against OpenAI and Microsoft, alleging the technology companies

item.title: Exclusive: Polls: Voters favor federal oversight of prediction markets
item.description:
Forty-eight percent of Republicans favor federal oversight, per the Republican poll.

🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 364 · likes: 2073

item.title: Anthropic's Red Lines Are No Substitute for Public Law
item.description:
The right response to democratic dysfunction is not to cede governance to private corporations, argues David Gantt.

reposts: 9 · likes: 29

item.title: Judge allows US search warrant targeting executive's AI chatbot records
item.description:
A federal judge has denied a bid by a business executive charged in a securities fraud prosecution to block a search warrant seeking data from ChatGPT maker OpenAI, rejecting his arguments that the request would ​sweep in protected communications with his defense lawyers.

reposts: 9 · likes: 20

item.title: Legal tech firm sues US over order limiting foreign access to top-tier Anthropic models
item.description:
A U.S. legal technology company on Tuesday sued the federal government, challenging a directive by President Donald Trump’s administration that resulted in ​the artificial intelligence firm Anthropic halting access to two of its ‌most advanced models for users worldwide.

reposts: 6 · likes: 20

item.title: Gas stations are using AI to inflate prices, new lawsuit alleges
item.description:
A new federal lawsuit alleges that gas station companies across California are engaged in an illegal conspiracy, powered by AI software, to raise prices.

🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 175 · likes: 452

item.title: First Amendment Questions for AI Transparency Laws
item.description:
State regulations mandating AI firms to disclose information may run afoul of the First Amendment. California is an early test case.

reposts: 5 · likes: 60

item.title: Slew Of California Gas Stations Illegally Used AI To Raise Prices, Lawsuit Claims
item.description:
The lawsuit cites a bill signed into law last year banning the use of common pricing algorithms.

🔥🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 57 · likes: 143

item.title: Workday must face California lawsuit over AI bias in job screening tools
item.description:
Workday must face claims that its popular AI-powered human resources software weeded out job applicants at other companies in ways that violated California law and a ​federal ban on discrimination against workers with disabilities, a federal judge ruled on ‌Monday.

🔥 · reposts: 20 · likes: 45

item.title: Trump’s Chaotic AI and Cybersecurity Policy
item.description:
The administration’s gutting of agencies, contradictory impulses on regulating AI, and susceptibility to outside interests undermine trust in its capacity to oversee artificial intelligence risks.

🔥🔥 · reposts: 16 · likes: 35

item.title: Why states are walking back their own climate and energy laws, and what they could do instead
item.description:
Many steps that are good for clean energy also dovetail with federal priorities, from affordable housing to data centers and rural development.

🔥 · reposts: 13 · likes: 28

item.title: Did the US Government Just Set An AI Export Precedent by Blocking Mythos?
item.description:
The government's real decision isn't about one model. It's about which legal theory the government uses to govern all of them, writes Joseph Hoefer.

reposts: 8 · likes: 16

item.title: New global order: AI CEOs as heads of nation-states
item.description:
The world's most powerful heads of state gathered this week for the annual G7 summit, with AI CEOs seated and treated like heads of nation-states themselves.

item.description:

item.title: Banning Open Source AI Would Be A Mistake
item.description:
